findings.title: Derived-consume reunion: callee holds the live token AND a &uniq borrow of its issuer; the loan link is erased at the call (LOAN-6a + LOAN-3's own exception)
findings.detail: Every step is accepted under the draft's own texts and its own precedents (C8 sanctions issue/mutate ops on reference-parameter bindings; C12 sanctions own-confined params whose in-body loan state is empty). The caller-side mint of &uniq tt is not a permissive misreading: LOAN-3's uniq clause carries the exception explicitly, and the call statement does consume the loan's holder per LOAN-6(a). The soundness sketch's suspension argument ('the callee cannot persist the token') misses that the callee does not need to persist anything — it receives the issuer alongside the token in the same statement, and LOAN-6(c) checks the body from an EMPTY loan state, so the issuer/token linkage is dropped at the ABI boundary. Invariant I is false for the whole call extent. Fix: LOAN-6(c) must seed the body-entry loan state from the signature — for every form-typed reference parameter C and every confined parameter h whose brand parameters unify, start with (kind(h), h) in loans(C). Then t.insert rejects inside gut (uniq loan live, statement does not consume e), while the legitimate body order e.fill(v); t.insert(...) still checks (consume clears the seeded loan). C8, C12, P4 are unaffected.

findings.title: Derived-reissue laundering: body mutates the issuer then returns the ORIGINAL token as the 'reissue'; caller's books stay perfectly balanced (LOAN-6b/6c)
findings.detail: This is the pure signature-laundering form: the derived caller-side effect (reissue) is exactly right as bookkeeping, yet the value bound as the 'fresh' holder is the stale original token, because nothing in the body judgment forces the returned confined value to be issued AFTER the body's last mutation of the issuer. LOAN-6(c)'s claim that 'LOAN-8 at its returns is what makes the derived caller-side effect sound' is demonstrated false — LOAN-8 checks region escape only, not interior validity ordering. Note the trusted form op cv.wait has the same signature shape but trusted internals; LOAN-6 extends the same privilege to untrusted bodies with no compensating check. The same fix as the previous finding closes this: with the body checked under seeded loans(t)={(uniq,e)}, t.insert is rejected unless e is first consumed, and once e is consumed it cannot be returned, so a returned Entry must be freshly issued after any mutation. The two FATALs are one root cause: helper-boundary loan-state erasure in LOAN-6(c).

findings.title: LOAN-4's carve-out contradicts LOAN-3's shr clause; the LOAN-4 reading hands out &uniq while a sibling shared cursor is live
findings.detail: The two normative texts disagree. LOAN-3's uniq clause carries an explicit consume-statement exception; its shr clause does not — but LOAN-4 grants a blanket carve-out 'despite LOAN-3' for any consuming/reissuing statement whose signature declares an issuer borrow. Under the LOAN-4 reading this program is accepted and dangles c2 entirely at the caller (no body inspection needed); under strict LOAN-3-shr it is rejected. P4 only needs the uniq-clause exception, so the safe resolution exists, but the draft must say it. Note the same shape is declarable in the form table (e.g. a close-with-compaction op taking &uniq self plus own Cursor), and LOAN-12's review obligations never mention sibling-loan compatibility for consume ops with &uniq receivers. Fix: restrict the carve-out (in both LOAN-3 and LOAN-4) to statements that surrender the SOLE loan on that issuer — automatic for uniq loans, and exactly the condition that protects siblings for shr.

findings.title: Reissue-on-loanless-param plus unconstrained brand instantiation at captureless construction launders a real fresh issue into no loan at all
findings.detail: Two unstated gaps compound. (1) LOAN-6(b)'s reissue arm matches purely on (region, brand) parameters and takes priority over the reference-parameter arm; it never requires the matched own param to actually hold a loan, so a loanless token (None) shadows the true derivation (fresh issue on tt) — and a checker that resolves 'reissue on the issuer of a nonexistent loan' as a no-op accepts the dangle above. (2) Brand parameters of captureless confined constructions are free generic arguments — brand forgery — which is what makes none well-typed at MaybeTok['r_tt, b_tt]. Reject-when-unsure would save this only if written down. Fix: (i) LOAN-6(b)'s reissue arm applies only when the matched own confined parameter holds a loan at the call site; otherwise fall through to the reference-parameter arm (or reject); (ii) a confined construction may instantiate its brand parameter only from a captured borrow's brand (equivalently: captureless constructions of brand-parameterized confined types are rejected).

findings.title: No kind/field-mode coherence: a confined(shr) type with a &uniq field yields coexisting shared tokens that alias unique write paths, breaking OWN-5 and the optimizer's shr-window fact
findings.detail: Form-op issues take their loan kind from the confined type's declared kind (CONF-1), while constructions take kind from the captured borrow mode (LOAN-2) — so the declared kind and the interior access mode can diverge, and only the construction path is self-consistent. With a shr-declared, &uniq-field type, two live tokens both mint unique projections into the same resolved place: OWN-5's exclusivity is violated single-threaded, and the soundness sketch's claim that 'while only shr loans are live, only &-receiver operations run' is false as a fact channel (interior mutation happens inside a shr-only window), which is miscompile-grade for an optimizer consuming it. LOAN-11's final clause happens to stop the par replication of uniq-LOAN holders, but this token holds a shr loan, so 'shr-backed' screening is also confused by the same incoherence. Fix is one CONF-1 sentence: a confined type with any (transitively reached) &uniq field must be declared confined(uniq); construction kind, declared kind, and field modes must agree.

findings.title: Loans and brands are undefined for form values not directly let-bound: any form owned by a struct field is either loan-dead (expressivity cliff) or handled by improvised, unspecified place rules
findings.detail: LOAN-1 attaches loan sets to bindings only, and LOAN-7 mints brands only at direct form lets, but nothing restricts issue/consume receivers to direct bindings. Every completion is bad: (a) strict fail-closed rejects all loanable ops on any form stored in any aggregate — SQLite-/mimalloc-style aggregate states in the frozen corpus become unwritable, an architecture-level expressivity wall the six canonical programs hide because every container in them is a direct local; (b) an implementer who improvises per-place loans without also specifying root-binding freeze and brand propagation gets unsoundness (e.g. keying the loan on the place db.t leaves loans(db) empty, so LOAN-3 permits 'let db2 = move db' while e is live, relocating the owner under a live token). The draft must state the rule: an operation on a place attaches its loan to the ROOT binding, kind as declared, with the root's brand extended by the projection path — or explicitly reject projected receivers and accept the cliff.

findings.title: LOAN-12's &-receiver obligation (interior-address preservation) is too weak for LOAN-11 replication: an address-preserving but internally mutating read op races under par
findings.detail: The checker's story for replicate-shared slots leans on LOAN-12's declaration-time obligation, but that obligation is stated purely as address preservation, which a memoizing/self-organizing read (cache, LRU counters, splay-on-read) satisfies while being racy under concurrent invocation. Effect rows do not catch it either: the op is declared reads-only in the form table (the lie IS the bug, and the stated review criterion does not ask the question that would expose it). This is on the declared trusted surface, so it is an obligation-wording defect rather than a checker-accepted dangle, but the accepted program above plus a form table that passes the written review races. Fix: strengthen the obligation — an op may be declared with & receiver mode on a loanable form only if it preserves interior addresses AND is safe under concurrent invocation (or introduce a separate sync mark that LOAN-11 requires of every form reachable through a replicate-shared slot).

findings.title: Zero-region loanable confined types slip LOAN-8: region escape is checked, brand escape is not
findings.detail: LOAN-8's whole guarantee ('a holder's region is its issuer's region, so every holder's scope lies within its issuer's scope') is carried by region parameters that the rule never requires to exist, and LOAN-8 checks only regions, never brands. The affine queue-endpoint instance in the M1 list invites exactly this region-free shape. The attack likely dies on a strict reading of LOAN-2's 'instantiated with C's region and brand' (a type with no region slot cannot be so instantiated), which is why this is MINOR — but the draft should not depend on a charitable reading of an instantiation phrase. Fix: CONF-1 requires every loanable confined type to carry at least one region parameter which LOAN-2 instantiates with the issuer's region; optionally extend LOAN-8 to require returned brands to be function brand parameters, making the guarantee doubly held.

findings.title: Plain move of a holder into a fresh let: holder rename is unspecified; one natural completion silently loses the release
findings.detail: Not a dangling-token break — the bad completion loses the form's declared release (a mutex that is never unlocked, a cursor never closed) and wedges loans(m) forever (fail-frozen, not fail-open) — but it is a hole in the holder-identity bookkeeping that LOAN-9/LOAN-10's name-sensitive equality then reasons over, so it should not be left to the implementer. Fix is one clause in LOAN-5: a plain let-move of a live holder renames its loan's holder to the new binding (matching the constructor-field case).

findings.title: LOAN-5 forbids destructuring any loan-holding confined value, so the flagship occupied/vacant entry API cannot be written as a match
findings.detail: Under the soundness lens this is the restrictive side of a load-bearing rule (extracting interior borrows must not create untracked holders), and it is what keeps the design sound against my match-arm extraction attacks — but it means the first-listed M1 instance, hash-table entry tokens, cannot take their canonical enum form in a match-only-conditional language; the draft's P2 quietly substitutes a fill-only token, and the fourteen frozen Hashbrown/SQLite operations will hit this wall. Fix direction if the corpus demands it: permit destructuring a loan-holding confined ENUM when, in each arm, exactly one bound piece carries all borrow/confined fields, renaming the loan's holder to that piece per arm, with LOAN-9 enforcing agreement at the join — one added rule, same no-flow character.

verdict: NOT sound as written. Two FATAL accepted-UAF programs (gut, refresh) break it at exactly the seam the M1 constraints made load-bearing — helper functions with signature-derived loan effects: LOAN-6(c) checks bodies from an EMPTY loan state, so a callee can hold a live token and a signature-declared &uniq borrow of the same issuer simultaneously (LOAN-3's own consume-statement exception mints the borrow), mutate, and either use the token or return it as a laundered 'reissue' with the caller's books perfectly balanced. Budget: claimed 15, self-admitted 17-19 at adversarial OWN-granularity — over the 15 target, under the 25 hard fail, and the repairs fit (~+2 rules). The single change that matters most: seed the LOAN-6(c) body-entry loan state from the signature — for every form-typed reference parameter C and confined parameter h with unifying brand parameters, start with (kind(h), h) in loans(C) — and narrow the LOAN-3/LOAN-4 consume carve-out to statements surrendering the SOLE loan on the issuer. That one repair closes both FATALs and the LOAN-3(shr)/LOAN-4 contradiction while preserving P4, C8, C12 and the wrapped-writer requirement. Secondary must-fixes before any YES: loan-holding precondition on LOAN-6(b)'s reissue arm plus brand-instantiation restriction on captureless constructions; kind/field-mode coherence in CONF-1; a stated rule (or stated rejection) for forms owned by fields; concurrency-strength wording in LOAN-12. Credit where due: the core no-flow machinery survived attack — LOAN-9/10 name-sensitive equality defeated cross-arm same-name reissue and loop-carried token tricks, and per-let brands plus the move freeze defeated every brand-staleness program I built — so the architecture is repairable, not dead.
